label: Transformers

node.label: Transformers
node.definition: Attention-centric residual networks process sequences with parallel content-based interaction.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Transformers is modeled as depending on Self-attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms and feeding downstream nodes such as Encoder–decoder transformers, Decoder-only causal transformers, Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models.
examples: atlas downstream link to Encoder–decoder transformers
atlas downstream link to Decoder-only causal transformers
atlas downstream link to Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Self-attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms.

dependencies.rationale: Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Self-attention.
In this atlas graph, Transformers has a `composes` dependency on Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms with `core` strength.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Residual, skip & highway connections.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Normalization & standardization.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
